name: Marketmore 76
image_alt: Dark green slicing cucumbers on a vine
description: The benchmark slicing cucumber — uniform, dark green, straight 8–9 inch fruits with crisp flesh, small seed cavity, and mild flavor. Bred for disease resistance and reliability.
history: Developed by Henry Munger at Cornell University and released in 1976. Bred to combine the classic flavor of heirloom slicing cucumbers with resistance to the major cucumber diseases. Became the standard for home and market gardens.
seasonal_quirks: Needs consistent heat and moisture — drought stress causes bitterness. Very sensitive to frost. Highly productive once established, producing for 6–8 weeks. Powdery mildew resistance extends the season significantly.
harvest_cues: Harvest at 7–8 inches, dark green and firm. Yellow skin indicates overripe — flavor becomes bitter and seeds tough. Check daily at peak production.
variety_notes: The disease resistance package — scab, mosaic, and downy mildew resistant — makes this significantly more reliable than non-resistant heirlooms in humid climates.
preservation: Bread and butter pickles. Dill pickles. Refrigerator pickles.
